sourceMovie.title: Awakenings
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Awakenings (1990) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Penny Marshall's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Awakenings clicked for you, One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest (1975) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bird Box
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Awakenings clicked for you, Bird Box (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Awakenings?
faq.answer: Try One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest, Bird Box, Pride & Prejudice—each shares DNA with Awakenings while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Awakenings?
faq.answer: Awakenings (1990) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Awakenings worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
